verses.book: The Apology of Rufinus. Addressed to Apronianus, in Reply to Jerome's Letter to Pammachius.
verses.text: The following is an epitome of the argument:

verses.book: The Apology of Rufinus. Addressed to Apronianus, in Reply to Jerome's Letter to Pammachius.
verses.text: I must submit to the taunts of my adversary as Christ did to those of the Jews.

verses.book: The Apology of Rufinus. Addressed to Apronianus, in Reply to Jerome's Letter to Pammachius.
verses.text: Yet the substantial charges must be answered.

verses.book: The Apology of Rufinus. Addressed to Apronianus, in Reply to Jerome's Letter to Pammachius.
verses.text: I praised him but he has wounded me.

verses.book: The Apology of Rufinus. Addressed to Apronianus, in Reply to Jerome's Letter to Pammachius.
verses.text: I am no heretic, but declare my faith, that of my baptism.

verses.book: The Apology of Rufinus. Addressed to Apronianus, in Reply to Jerome's Letter to Pammachius.
verses.text: I give a further proof of my faith in the resurrection of the flesh.

verses.book: The Apology of Rufinus. Addressed to Apronianus, in Reply to Jerome's Letter to Pammachius.
verses.text: The resurrection body is a spiritual body.
